nodes.name: Categorise request based on transaction types

values.content:
=Customer ticket title: {{ $('Set request data test').item.json.translated_data.translations.title || $('Set request data test').item.json.original_ticket_content.title}}
Customer ticket description: {{ $('Set request data test').item.json.translated_data.translations.description || $('Set request data test').item.json.original_ticket_content.description}}

Your task is to determine whether the customer explicitly requested a full refund, meaning a refund that covers more than one transaction/order/charge etc.

You must answer only this question: Did the customer explicitly request a full refund covering multiple transactions/orders/charges?

Common phrases that indicate TRUE
"full refund"
“I want a full refund.”
“Please issue a full refund for everything.”
“Refund all charges on my account.”
“Refund all transactions.”
“Refund everything I paid.”
“Cancel and refund all of it.”


Return FALSE if:
The customer requests an upsell-only refund (single add-on) or a single order refund.
The customer asks for a refund of last charge or transaction.
The customer is only asking about refund policy/eligibility/timing without explicitly requesting a full refund.
The message is ambiguous about scope (e.g., “I want a refund” or "please refund" or simply just "refund" with no indication it covers multiple transactions). In ambiguity, return FALSE.

Key Trigger Requirements (must be explicit)

To return TRUE, the ticket must clearly express:
	1.	a request for a refund, AND
	2.	that the scope is full / everything / all, OR explicitly references multiple transactions/orders/charges (e.g., “all charges,” “multiple orders,” “main + upsells,” “everything I paid”).

Classification Rules
	•	Respond TRUE only if the customer explicitly requests a full refund covering multiple transactions/orders/charges or they requested a full refund
	•	Otherwise respond FALSE.
	•	Do not infer intent beyond the text.
	•	If unclear, respond FALSE.

Output Format

Return a valid JSON object only:
{
“full_refund”: true or false
}

values.content:
=Customer Support Agent's comment: {{ $json["Customer reply "] }}

Original language: {{ $('Set request data test1').item.json.translated_data.originalLanguage }}

You are a translation engine. Your sole task is to translate Customer Support Agent's messages into the customer's language. 

Input:
Customer Support Agent's comment - this needs to be translated
Original language - this is the language you need to translate into

Rules (must be followed strictly):
Translate exactly and literally
Do not add, remove, infer, or reinterpret any information
Do not explain, summarize, rephrase, or improve wording
Preserve tone, intent, ambiguity, and emotional content exactly
Preserve formatting, punctuation, emojis, HTML formatting, and line breaks
Do not normalize slang, profanity, or errors
If a term cannot be translated precisely, transliterate it
If the input is partially English, translate only the non-English parts

Output needs to contain:
translatedComment: string

Needs to be formatted as JSON
No commentary, labels, or notes
No markdown or HTML unless it exists in the input

parameters.content: # No upsell transactions

## Status: solved
## Category: Refund>upsell

values.content:
You are a classifier that analyzes customer support tickets.

Your task is to determine whether the customer explicitly requested a refund of their upsell purchase or upsell transaction.

REQUIREMENT
Only classify as TRUE if the customer 
1. explicitly uses the word “upsell” in their request.
2. or they mention that they want a refund for "Personal Growth", "Career Guidance", or "Self-Esteem"

Do NOT infer an upsell based on pricing, subscriptions, upgrades, or context.
If one of the following “upsell”, "Personal Growth", "Career Guidance", or "Self-Esteem" is not present, the answer must be FALSE.

Examples of valid upsell refund requests (TRUE)
“I want a refund of my upsell.”
“Please refund my upsell purchase.”
“I was charged for the upsell, please refund it.”
“I don’t want the upsell, I want a refund.”
"I want a refund for career guidance"
"I want a refund of personal growth transaction"

Examples that must be FALSE
“I was charged monthly, please refund.”
“Cancel my subscription and refund me.”
“I didn’t mean to upgrade, refund please.”
“Refund the full charge.”
(These do NOT explicitly mention “upsell” or any of the phrases mentioned above)


Output Format

Return a valid JSON object with:

{
"upsell_refund_requested": true or false
}

## Upsell only request
## Category:  refund upsell
## Status: Solved

original_ticket_content.title: Urgent: Unauthorized Subscription Charge – Refund & Cancellation Request
original_ticket_content.description:
Hi John,  Thanks for getting back to me.  Here are the details you requested:   Email address linked to the account:
marcher_carob.5l@icloud.com  Date the subscription started / transaction date:
16 June  Last four digits of the payment card:
I used Apple ID / Apple Pay, so Apple may have masked the card details. The card may appear under one of the last four-digit numbers shown in the attached screenshots.  Picture of the latest charge:
Please see the attached screenshots showing the pending and past transactions.   Could you please use this information to locate the account adn cancel the subscription immediately, & confirm that no further charges will be made?  Kind regards  Rahan
